milestoneTitle: Phase M9.18b.3 — Fix the REAL first-load crash. Hoisted PIN_SVG_OUTLINE / PIN_SVG_FILLED / MASTERY_SVG_OUTLINE / MASTERY_SVG_FILLED / TRASH_SVG / GRAB_SVG (from ~L1068-1105) and TRACK_LABEL (from ~L1696) to the top-of-IIFE hoisted state block (~L306-330). Reason: renderDashLearn and renderContinueCard reference all 7 consts during the initial applyFilter('home') -> replayHomeAnimations call at L602. M9.18a masked the bug because slice.map iterated over an empty lessonsData array (no tile rendered, no const read). M9.18b's dummy-fallback tile made those reads fire first-load, hitting the TDZ and aborting the entire IIFE — which silently killed data-chip click handlers, loadLessons, and every downstream wire-up. User symptom: dashboard non-functional on first load, content only appearing after refreshing on a non-home route (because refresh on #learn skips replayHomeAnimations and lets the IIFE complete). This is M9.18b.1's cousin bug — same TDZ pattern, different consts. CLAUDE.md TDZ rule holds: any module-level const read from a render fn during initial applyFilter must live above L602.
